failed_cases.description: Public names import, default Markdown is callable, and explicit renderers construct through the public API.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown, HTMLRenderer, ASTRenderer, escape_html
m = Markdown()
print(callable(m), hasattr(m, 'parse'), hasattr(m, 'register_inline'), hasattr(m, 'register_block'))
print(type(Markdown(renderer=HTMLRenderer()).renderer).__name__)
print(Markdown(renderer='ast')('plain')[0]['type'])
print(escape_html('"<&>'))
failed_cases.expected_output:
True True True True
HTMLRenderer
paragraph
"<&>
failed_cases.expected:
True True True True

paragraph
"<&>

               ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

failed_cases.description: Block parsing recognizes fenced code blocks with an optional language.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
t = Markdown('ast').parse('```python\nprint("<x>")\n```')[0]
print(t['type'], t.get('lang'), t.get('text'))

failed_cases.description: Block parsing recognizes indented code and thematic breaks.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
tokens = Markdown('ast').parse('    a < b\n    c\n\n---')
print(tokens[0]['type'], tokens[0]['text'].startswith('a < b\nc'))
print(tokens[1]['type'])

failed_cases.description: Inline parsing handles emphasis and strong emphasis.

from minimarkdown import Markdown
children = Markdown('ast')('*em* and __strong__')[0]['children']
print([c['type'] for c in children])
print(children[0]['children'][0]['text'], children[2]['children'][0]['text'])

failed_cases.description: AST renderer returns public token dictionaries with nested children and without private state.
failed_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
tokens = Markdown('ast')('Text **bold** and `code`')
para = tokens[0]
print(type(tokens).__name__, para['type'])
print([c['type'] for c in para['children']])
print(any(k.startswith('_') for t in tokens for k in t.keys()))

failed_cases.description: Custom inline plugin callables can register parse and render handlers.

from minimarkdown import Markdown

def mark_plugin(md):
    md.register_inline('mark', r'==([^=]+)==', lambda m: {'text': m.group(1)}, lambda r, t: '<mark>' + r.render_inlines([{'type':'text','text':t['text']}]) + '</mark>')

Intro paragraph' in not_table) print('

' in yes_table, '' in yes_table)
12

all_cases.description: Public names import, default Markdown is callable, and explicit renderers construct through the public API.

print(Markdown(renderer='ast')('plain')[0]['type'])
print(escape_html('"<&>'))

from minimarkdown import Markdown
m = Markdown('ast')

print([(t['type'], t.get('level'), t.get('text')) for t in tokens])

all_cases.description: AST renderer returns public token dictionaries with nested children and without private state.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
tokens = Markdown('ast')('Text **bold** and `code`')
para = tokens[0]
print(type(tokens).__name__, para['type'])
print([c['type'] for c in para['children']])
print(any(k.startswith('_') for t in tokens for k in t.keys()))

all_cases.description: Custom inline plugin callables can register parse and render handlers.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown

def mark_plugin(md):
    md.register_inline('mark', r'==([^=]+)==', lambda m: {'text': m.group(1)}, lambda r, t: '<mark>' + r.render_inlines([{'type':'text','text':t['text']}]) + '</mark>')

print(Markdown(plugins=[mark_plugin])('A ==hit & run=='))

all_cases.description: Malformed inline constructs remain literal and unknown plugin names raise ValueError.
Heading and paragraph block text flows through inline emphasis and link parsing before HTML rendering.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown
print(Markdown()('# **Title**\n\nA *paragraph* with [link](https://e.test).'))

all_cases.description: Block quote inner content is reparsed as blocks and then as inline content.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown html = Markdown()('> ## Inner\n> quoted *text*\n>\n> - item **one**') print('
' in html, '

Inner

' in html) print('

quoted text

' in html) print('
  • item one
    • ' in html)

all_cases.description: Tight and loose list item state controls paragraph rendering while item prose still receives inline parsing.
all_cases.test_code:
from minimarkdown import Markdown html = Markdown()('- tight *one* - tight **two** - loose item continued *para*') print('
  • tight one
    • ' in html) print('
  • tight two
    • ' in html) print('

  • loose item

    continued para

    • ' in html)

all_cases.description: Code spans inside prose and fenced code blocks both escape HTML while suppressing nested inline parsing.
all_cases.test_code: from minimarkdown import Markdown html = Markdown()('Inline `****` here.\n\n```html\n& raw\n```') print('**<x>**' in html) print('& raw' in html) print('<strong>& raw</strong>' in html)

all_cases.description: Link labels can contain nested inline emphasis while href and title attributes are escaped independently.
all_cases.test_code: from minimarkdown import Markdown html = Markdown()('[*A & B*](https://e.test?q=1&v="x" "T & C")') print('A & B' in html) print('href="https://e.test?q=1&v="x""' in html) print('title="T & C"' in html)

from minimarkdown import Markdown m = Markdown(plugins=['table']) not_table = m('Intro paragraph\nA | B\n| --- | --- |') yes_table = m('| A | B |\n| --- | --- |\n| 1 | 2 |') print('' in not_table, '

Intro paragraph' in not_table) print('

' in yes_table, '' in yes_table)

all_cases.description: Malformed inline and plugin-looking syntax remains literal without poisoning later parses on the same instance.
all_cases.test_code: from minimarkdown import Markdown m = Markdown(plugins=['strikethrough']) first = m('Bad ~~strike and [link](oops then **ok**') second = m('Next ~~good~~ and *fine*') print('~~strike' in first, 'ok' in first) print(second)
